summary: Full 12-checkpoint E2E pre-launch test for The Doggy Company platform. Critical SyntaxError in mira_service_desk.py was found and fixed (mark_reply_read endpoint was accidentally placed inside HTML f-string). 22/22 backend API tests passed. 11/12 UI checkpoints PASS. CP4 (admin inbox) FAIL due to architectural mismatch between ticket collections.

critical_fix_applied.issue: mark_reply_read endpoint (lines 1303-1313) was placed INSIDE the HTML email f-string template in concierge_reply function. Python closed the f-string at the docstring triple-quotes on line 1305, causing SyntaxError that prevented the backend from starting.
critical_fix_applied.fix: Removed the mark_reply_read endpoint from inside the f-string and placed it correctly after the concierge_reply function (after line ~1333)
critical_fix_applied.verification: Backend starts OK, all 22 tests pass

checkpoint_results.CP1_LOGIN: PASS - dipali@clubconcierge.in logs in, lands on /pet-home with Mojo showing 100% soul score
checkpoint_results.CP2_MIRA_PICKS: PASS - Care page Mira's Picks tab shows 16 AI-scored products for Mojo (not loading spinner)
checkpoint_results.CP3_BOOKING: PASS - 'Book via Concierge' on service cards opens multi-step ConciergeModal, 'Send to Concierge for Mojo' works, success message shown
checkpoint_results.CP4_ADMIN_INBOX: FAIL - Admin Service Desk Workspace reads from /api/tickets/ (orders collection) NOT /api/service_desk/tickets (concierge intent collection). Admin cannot see member concierge booking tickets with Mira's briefing. See critical_code_review_comments for RCA.
checkpoint_results.CP5_MY_REQUESTS: PASS - /my-requests shows 4 tickets, ticket expands showing full Mira briefing with MIRA'S BRIEFING FOR CONCIERGE section
checkpoint_results.CP6_NEARME_BOOKING: PASS - /celebrate → searched 'Goa' → found NearMeConciergeModal with venue pre-filled (Noah's Park Animal Services) → date input + textarea work → Send shows 'Sent to your Concierge!' success
checkpoint_results.CP7_CONCIERGE_REPLY: PASS - POST /api/service_desk/concierge_reply returns success=True and has_unread_concierge_reply=True
checkpoint_results.CP8_UNREAD_BADGE: PASS - Pink 'New reply' dot visible on /my-requests ticket, clicking ticket calls mark_reply_read API and clears the badge
checkpoint_results.CP9_MIRA_CHAT: PASS - /mira-os has 'Ask Mira anything...' input, typed 'What should Mojo eat today?', received food recommendations. mira_chat_intent ticket exists in service_desk_tickets (TCK-2026-000006 subject='Mira Chat Intent for Mojo'). Note: Mira tab click on /my-requests is blocked by overlay but API shows ticket exists.
checkpoint_results.CP10_SOUL_SCORE: PASS - Soul ring shows 100%, all 6 chapter pills visible: Identity, Behaviour, Health, Social, Nutrition, Learning
checkpoint_results.CP11_SHOP_TABS: PASS - Shop /shop page has Celebrate and Care tabs with force-click. Celebrate shows birthday cakes/breed items, Care shows different products. Tab switching works.
checkpoint_results.CP12_FOOTER: PASS - Footer.jsx correctly shows +91 97399 08844 and woof@thedoggycompany.com

critical.issue: FIXED in this iteration: SyntaxError due to endpoint being placed inside HTML f-string template in concierge_reply function. Backend could not start.

minor.issue: Queue endpoint queries mira_conversations collection instead of service_desk_tickets. Returns 0 results for all queues.

Admin Service Desk Workspace reads from tickets collection (order tickets) while concierge intent tickets are in service_desk_tickets. Admin cannot see member concierge booking requests with Mira briefing.

ui_bugs.issue: Banner div with class 'bg-gradient-to-r from-purple-600 via-pink-500 to-orange-500' intercepts pointer events on buttons below it (shop tabs, navigation items). Buttons require force=True to click in Playwright.

integration_issues.issue: Admin Service Desk Workspace (DoggyServiceDesk.jsx) fetches from /api/tickets/ (order tickets). Concierge booking tickets from attach_or_create_ticket are stored in service_desk_tickets collection and NOT visible in admin inbox. Admin cannot see booking tickets with Mira briefing.
The 'Mira' tab button on /my-requests page times out when clicked via Playwright - blocked by overlay. Works in real browser. API confirms mira_chat_intent tickets exist.

issues: Email shows woof@thedoggycompany.in (line 193) — should be .com per CP12 requirement
Error messages reference woof@thedoggycompany.in (lines 1130, 1134) — should be .com
Multiple occurrences of woof@thedoggybakery.in — confirm if this bakery email is intentional

rca_of_admin_inbox_issue.root_cause: Two separate MongoDB collections for tickets: (1) 'tickets' collection for order/commerce tickets created by ticket_auto_creation.py, (2) 'service_desk_tickets' collection for Mira intent-based concierge booking tickets created by attach_or_create_ticket. The admin Service Desk Workspace only reads from 'tickets' collection. Also: ticket ID counter is shared (TCK-2026-XXXXXX) between both collections, creating potential ID conflicts.
files_to_fix: backend/mira_service_desk.py: attach_or_create_ticket should also write a summary to tickets collection OR
backend/mira_service_desk.py: get_tickets_by_queue (line 1085) should query service_desk_tickets NOT mira_conversations
rca_of_admin_inbox_issue.mitigation: Admin can view individual tickets via /api/service_desk/ticket/{ticket_id} directly, but no admin inbox view exists for concierge intent tickets

action_items: HIGH: Fix admin Service Desk Workspace to also show service_desk_tickets (concierge intent tickets with Mira briefing). Either make DoggyServiceDesk.jsx also fetch from /api/service_desk/tickets, or make attach_or_create_ticket write to the tickets collection too.
HIGH: Fix get_tickets_by_queue in mira_service_desk.py (line 1085) to query service_desk_tickets instead of mira_conversations
MEDIUM: Fix Contact.jsx line 193: woof@thedoggycompany.in → woof@thedoggycompany.com
MEDIUM: Fix MiraAI.jsx lines 1130,1134: woof@thedoggycompany.in → woof@thedoggycompany.com
LOW: Consider fixing the top gradient banner z-index/pointer-events to not intercept clicks on shop tab buttons
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL: Two ticket collection architecture (tickets vs service_desk_tickets) with shared ID counter creates ID collisions and disconnected admin view. E1 must unify or properly bridge these collections.
HIGH: mira_service_desk.py get_tickets_by_queue queries mira_conversations (line 1085) — should query service_desk_tickets for the admin queue view
MEDIUM: Contact.jsx and MiraAI.jsx still have .in email addresses
LOW: Backend mira_service_desk.py is 1512 lines — consider splitting into smaller modules (service_desk_api.py, concierge_reply.py, ticket_utils.py)
updated_files: /app/backend/mira_service_desk.py (fixed SyntaxError: moved mark_reply_read endpoint out of HTML f-string)

success_rate.frontend: 91.7% (11/12 checkpoints pass, CP4 admin inbox fails)

seed_data_creation: Backend tests created test booking ticket via attach_or_create_ticket (cleaned up with mark_reply_read test)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend SyntaxError in mira_service_desk.py was fixed in this iteration (mark_reply_read placed inside HTML f-string). All 22 API tests pass. CP4 admin inbox fails because DoggyServiceDesk.jsx reads /api/tickets/ (order tickets) while concierge booking tickets are in service_desk_tickets. E1 needs to bridge these collections. CP9 Mira chat works on /mira-os page. The top gradient banner intercepts click events - use force=True for shop tabs. /my-requests Mira tab overlay issue present but ticket data is correct per API.
